message:
fix(cards): InfoTooltip Escape uses topmost-handler stack (#6227) (#6251)

CardWrapper's InfoTooltip used to register a fresh document keydown
listener for every visible tooltip instance. With multiple cards on a
dashboard, pressing Escape would fire all 10 listeners and close every
open tooltip simultaneously. The same Escape press could also reach
DrillDownModal's keydown listener and trigger an unrelated close.

Fix: route InfoTooltip Escape handling through a shared module-level
LIFO stack:

- Each tooltip pushes its close callback when visible.
- Only the topmost (most recently opened) callback runs on Escape.
- A single document listener is attached on first push and removed on
  last pop, so closed tooltips don't pile up listeners.
- The shared handler calls stopImmediatePropagation() so peer keydown
  handlers like DrillDownModal don't run when a tooltip is the topmost
  open element.

Closes #6227

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
